div1.entryFree: Palate, vb. to perceive by the taste, to taste: not --ing the taste of her dishonour, Troil. IV, 1, 59. you are plebeians, if they be senators: and they are no less, when, both your voices blended, the greatest taste most --s theirs, Cor. III, 1, 104 (when the predominant taste of the compound is that of their voices). and never --s more the dung, Ant. V, 2, 7.
